samples.xml_description:
Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit. It receives a serial input signal "din_serial" along with a control signal "din_valid" indicating the validity of the input data. The module operates on the rising edge of the clock signal "clk" and uses a synchronous design. The input din_serial is a single-bit data, and when the module receives 8 input data, the output dout_parallel outputs the 8-bit data(The serial input values are sequentially placed in dout_parallel from the most significant bit to the least significant bit), and the dout_valid is set to 1. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). Serial input data. Validity signal for input data. Parallel output data. Validity signal for the output data. ADD SUB SRA SRL Valid output signal for the output data. Parallel output data. Counter for the number of serial input data bits received.

On each rising edge of the clock, if the reset signal is active, the counter is reset to 0. If the reset signal is inactive, the counter is incremented by 1 if the input data is valid.

When the counter reaches 8, the counter is reset to 0, and the valid output signal is set to 1. If the counter is not 8, the valid output signal is set to 0.

On each rising edge of the clock, if the reset signal is active, the parallel output is also reset to 0. If the reset signal is inactive, the parallel output is updated to include the serial input data at the current count position if the input data is valid.

In summary, the module receives serial input data and counts the number of input data bits received. When the counter reaches 8, the counter is reset to 0, and the valid output signal is set to 1. The parallel output is updated to include the serial input data at the current count position if the input data is valid.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. Clock. Reset. 1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0). 8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division. 8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division. 1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present. 1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged. 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready. 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits. 0 1 2

Initialize the divider:

  • When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.
  • The shift register (SR) is initialized with the absolute value of the dividend, shifted left by one bit.
  • NEG_DIVISOR is set to the negated absolute value of the divisor.
  • The counter (cnt) is initialized to 1 and start_cnt is set to 1 to begin the division process.

Division process:

  • When start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps:
  • When cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete.
  • cnt and start_cnt are cleared.
  • The shift register SR is updated with the final remainder and quotient.
  • Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result:
  • The subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR is computed.
  • The carry-out is determined using a multiplexer.
  • The shift register SR is updated by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.

Result validity:

  • res_valid is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.
  • res_valid is set to zero if rst is high.
  • When cnt reaches 8, res_ready is set to 1.
  • Otherwise, if res_valid and res_ready is all high, res_valid is set to 0.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a data width conversion circuit that converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output. The module provides two output ports: valid_out, which indicates the validity of the output data, and data_out, which represents the converted 16-bit output data. The first arriving 8-bit data should be placed in the higher 8 bits of the 16-bit data output. The valid_out and data_out signals are generated in the next clock cycle after the two data inputs. When there is only one data input, valid_out and data_out are not generated immediately. Instead, they wait for the arrival of the next data input to complete the concatenation of the two data inputs before generating valid_out and data_out. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input signal indicating the validity of the input data. 8-bit input data to be converted. Output signal indicating the validity of the output data. 16-bit output data resulting from the width conversion. 0 1 2 1'b0 8'b0 8'b0 1'b0 16'b0

On reset, state is set to state_idle, and both data_lock and valid_out_reg are cleared to 0.

When valid_in is high, if state is state_idle, the incoming data_in is stored in data_lock. If state is state_1st, the incoming data_in is stored in data_lock_1. If state is state_2nd, valid_out_reg is set to 1 and data_out_reg is set to the concatenation of data_lock and data_lock_1.

When valid_in is low, if state is state_2nd, state is set to state_idle.

samples.xml_description:
<module id="pe">
    <description>Implement a Multiplying Accumulator for 32bit integer. In the MAC_PE, there is a register that stores the partial sum (the intermediate accumulation result), and in each cycle, the result of “a multiplied by b” will be accumulated in this register, and the “c” shows the value of the register.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal. Defined as 1 for reset and 0 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="c" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit output representing the accumulated result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="MAC_PE">Multiply and accumulate in a PE.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Accumulator Register: The module includes a register, c, to store the accumulated result. On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the positive edge of the reset signal (rst), the module updates the register. If the reset signal (rst) is high, indicating a reset condition, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the register (c) is updated by adding the product of the input operands (a and b) to its current value. Multiplication and Accumulation: Inside the always block, the module performs the multiplication and accumulation operation. If the reset signal (rst) is high, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the module adds the product of the input operands (a and b) to the register (c).</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal conditions, the motor vehicle lane light should have a period of 60 clock cycles of green, 5 clock cycles of yellow, and 10 clock cycles of red. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it is shortened to 10 clocks. If it is less than 10 clocks, it remains unchanged. The lane light and the sidewalk light should be paired. When the lane light is green or yellow, the sidewalk light is red. When the lane light is red, the sidewalk light is green. For simplicity, only the lane light is considered. Reset signal (active low). Clock signal. Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass. Red signal output. Yellow signal output. Green signal output. Idle state State 1 - Red light State 2 - Yellow light State 3 - Green light The state transition logic is implemented using a case statement. The state transitions are as follows:
  • idle: Transitions to s1_red state.
  • s1_red: Transitions to s3_green state when the counter reaches 60.
  • s2_yellow: Transitions to s1_red state when the counter reaches 60.
  • s3_green: Transitions to s2_yellow state when the counter reaches 60.
The state transition logic is independent of the inputs and only depends on the current state. The counter is used to determine when to transition to the next state. case (state) idle: begin if (1) state = s1_red; else state = idle; end s1_red: begin if (cnt == 60) state = s3_green; else state = s1_red; end s2_yellow: begin if (cnt == 60) state = s1_red; else state = s2_yellow; end s3_green: begin if (cnt == 60) state = s2_yellow; else state = s3_green; end endcase The counter is used to count the number of clock cycles. The counter is reset to 10 when rst_n is low. The counter is set to 60 when the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active. The counter is set to 60 when the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) is active. The counter is set to 5 when the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) is active. The counter is set to 10 when the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) is active. Otherwise, the counter is decremented by 1. if (!rst_n) cnt = 10; else if (pass_request && green) cnt = 60; else if (!green && p_green) cnt = 60; else if (!yellow && p_yellow) cnt = 5; else if (!red && p_red) cnt = 10; else cnt = cnt - 1; The output signals are updated based on the previous state of the state machine. The output signals are updated based on the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. if (!rst_n) {red, yellow, green} = {p_red, p_yellow, p_green}; else {red, yellow, green} = {p_red, p_yellow, p_green};

samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 8-bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock. Active-low reset. Input enable. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Output enable. Product. 8 Reset the internal pipeline registers. always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (!rst_n) begin mul_en_out_reg <= 0; mul_a_reg <= 0; mul_b_reg <= 0; end end Update the internal pipeline registers. always @(posedge clk) begin mul_en_out_reg <= mul_en_in; mul_a_reg <= mul_a; mul_b_reg <= mul_b; end Generate the partial products. genvar i; generate for (i = 0; i < N; i = i + 1) begin : gen_partial_products assign temp[i] = mul_a_reg && {N{mul_b_reg[i]}}; end endgenerate Calculate the partial sums. genvar j; generate for (j = 0; j < N-1; j = j + 1) begin : gen_partial_sums if (j == 0) begin assign sum[j] = temp[j] + temp[j+1]; end else begin assign sum[j] = sum[j-1] + temp[j+1]; end end endgenerate Calculate the final product. assign mul_out = mul_en_out_reg ? sum[N-2] : 0;

samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Converts parallel input data to serial output.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal for serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="cnt" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[2:0]">Counter register.</reg>
        <reg id="data" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[3:0]">Data register.</reg>
        <reg id="valid" type="sequential_logic">Valid signal register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <para>When the reset signal (rst_n) is low, the counter register (cnt) is reset to 0.</para>
        <para>When the reset signal (rst_n) is high, if the counter register (cnt) is 3 (indicating the last bit of the parallel input), the data register (data) is updated with the parallel input (d), the counter register (cnt) is reset to 0, and the valid signal (valid) is set to 1.</para>
        <para>Otherwise, the counter register (cnt) is incremented by 1, the valid signal (valid) is set to 0, and the data register (data) is shifted left by 1 bit.</para>
        <para>The valid signal (valid_out) indicates the availability of serial output. When the counter register (cnt) is 3, the valid signal (valid) is set to 1, indicating that the serial output is available. Otherwise, the valid signal (valid) is set to 0.</para>
        <para>The serial data output (dout) is the most significant bit of the data register (data).</para>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit dividend.</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit divisor.</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit quotient.</output>
        <output id="odd" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit remainder.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH" value="16">Bit width of the dividend and quotient.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DIV_WIDTH" value="8">Bit width of the divisor.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed.</implementation>
</module>

Initialize shifts to 0.

After shifts reaches 8, reset shifts to 0.

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Implement a Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="S0">Initial state of the FSM.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S1">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S2">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S3">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S4">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S5">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S6">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S7">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S8">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">Current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="sequential_logic">Next state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="match_reg" type="sequential_logic">Register to store the MATCH output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <always description="Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of CLK or the negative edge of RST">
                <if description="Reset condition">
                    <condition description="RST is active">
                        <true>
                            <assign description="Reset the state to S0">state = S0;</assign>
                            <assign description="Reset the MATCH output to 0">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                        </true>
                    </condition>
                </if>
                <else description="If RST is not active">
                    <true>
                        <assign description="Update the state to the next state">state = next_state;</assign>
                    </true>
                </else>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <combinational_logic>
            <always description="Combinational logic block triggered on the change of IN or state">
                <if description="Input condition">
                    <condition description="IN is 1">
                        <true>
                            <case description="Case statement based on the current state">
                                <condition description="State S0">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S1">next_state = S1;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S1">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S2;">next_state = S2;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S2">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S3;">next_state = S3;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S3">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S4;">next_state = S4;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S4">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S1;">next_state = S1;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 1;">match_reg = 1;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S1">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S2;">next_state = S2;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S2">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S3;">next_state = S3;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S3">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S4;">next_state = S4;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S4">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S1;">next_state = S1;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                            </case>
                        </true>
                    </condition>
                    <else description="If IN is not 1">
                        <true>
                            <case description="Case statement based on the current state">
                                <condition description="State S0">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S0;">next_state = S0;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S1">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S0;">next_state = S0;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S2">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S0;">next_state = S0;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S3">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S0;">next_state = S0;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                                <condition description="State S4">
                                    <true>
                                        <assign description="Update the next state to S0;">next_state = S0;</assign>
                                        <assign description="Update the MATCH output to 0;">match_reg = 0;</assign>
                                    </true>
                                </condition>
                            </case>
                        </true>
                    </else>
                </if>
            </always>
        </combinational_logic>
        <assign description="Assign MATCH output to match_reg">MATCH = match_reg;</assign>
    </implementation>
</module>

        <parameter id="CLK_1">CLK_1</parameter>

    <implementation>Reset the counter cnt_100 to 0 when the reset signal RST is active. Toggle the output clock signal CLK_1 when the counter cnt_100 reaches a value of 49. Increment the counter cnt_100 by 1 on every positive edge of CLK_in. Reset the counter cnt_10 to 0 when the reset signal RST is active. Reset the counter cnt_50 to 0 when the reset signal RST is active. Toggle the output clock signal CLK_50 when the counter cnt_50 reaches a value of 24. <module id="alu">
    <description>Implement an ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">ALU control signal.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">A 1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">A 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">A 1-bit output representing a general flag, which is set based on specific operations (SLT and SLTU).</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="ADD">6'b100000</parameter>
        <parameter id="ADDU">6'b100001</parameter>
        <parameter id="SUB">6'b100010</parameter>
        <parameter id="SUBU">6'b100011</parameter>
        <parameter id="AND">6'b100100</parameter>
        <parameter id="OR">6'b100101</parameter>
        <parameter id="XOR">6'b100110</parameter>
        <parameter id="NOR">6'b100111</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLT">6'b101010</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLTU">6'b101011</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLL">6'b000000</parameter>
        <parameter id="SRL">6'b000010</parameter>
        <parameter id="SRA">6'b000011</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLLV">6'b000100</parameter>
        <parameter id="SRLV">6'b000110</parameter>
        <parameter id="SRAV">6'b000111</parameter>
        <parameter id="LUI">6'b001111</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. 
The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).</implementation>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="state0">Initial state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state1">Detecting edges.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state2">Rising edge detected.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state3">Falling edge detected.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state">State variable.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="next_state">Next state variable.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="rise_reg">Rising edge output register.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="down_reg">Falling edge output register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. It uses a state machine to monitor changes in the input signal. On the positive edge of the clock, it updates the current state and the rising and falling edge output registers based on the input signal. If a rising edge is detected, the rise output is set to 1, and if a falling edge is detected, the down output is set to 1. The module resets to the initial state on a low reset signal.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplicand.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplier.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="size">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <task>
        <description>Extend input signals.</description>
        <parameter_description>
            <parameter name="i">Loop index.</parameter>
        </parameter_description>
        <implementation>
            <assign name="extension_a">{{size{1'b0}}, mul_a};</assign>
            <assign name="extension_b">{{size{1'b0}}, mul_b};</assign>
        </implementation>
    </task>
    <task>
        <description>Generate partial product.</description>
        <parameter_description>
            <parameter name="i">Loop index.</parameter>
        </parameter_description>
        <implementation>
            <assign name="partial_product">extension_a & {4{extension_b[i]}};</assign>
        </implementation>
    </task>
    <task>
        <description>Generate pipeline registers.</description>
        <parameter_description>
            <parameter name="i">Loop index.</parameter>
        </parameter_description>
        <implementation>
            <reg name="sum_reg">0</reg>
            <reg name="carry_reg">0</reg>
            <reg name="sum_reg_next">0</reg>
            <reg name="carry_reg_next">0</reg>
        </implementation>
    </task>
    <task>
        <description>Update registers.</description>
        <parameter_description>
            <parameter name="i">Loop index.</parameter>
        </parameter_description>
        <implementation>
            <assign name="sum_reg_next">sum_reg + partial_product + carry_reg;</assign>
            <assign name="carry_reg_next">sum_reg[i] & carry_reg | sum_reg[i] & partial_product[i] | carry_reg & partial_product[i];</assign>
        </implementation>
    </task>
    <task>
        <description>Update output.</description>
        <parameter_description>
            <parameter name="i">Loop index.</parameter>
        </parameter_description>
        <implementation>
            <assign name="mul_out">sum_reg_next + carry_reg_next;</assign>
        </implementation>
    </task>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="calendar">
    <description>Module name: calendar
    Input ports: CLK: Clock input
    RST: Active high reset signal
    Output ports: Hours: 6-bit output representing the current hours
    Mins: 6-bit output representing the current minutes
    Secs: 6-bit output representing the current seconds
    Implementation: The calendar module uses three always blocks to update the values of seconds, minutes, and hours based on the clock signal and reset signal.
    The first always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge CLK) or the positive edge of the reset signal (posedge RST). It checks if the reset signal is active (RST) and sets the seconds value (Secs) to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it wraps around and sets the seconds value to 0. Otherwise, it increments the seconds value by 1.
    The second always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the minutes value (Mins). If the reset signal is active, it sets the minutes value to 0. If both the minutes and seconds values are 59, it wraps around and sets the minutes value to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it increments the minutes value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the minutes value unchanged.
    The third always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the hours value (Hours). If the reset signal is active, it sets the hours value to 0. If the hours, minutes, and seconds values are all at their maximum (23, 59, and 59 respectively), it wraps around and sets the hours value to 0. If the minutes and seconds values are both 59, it increments the hours value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the hours value unchanged.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK">Clock input</input>
        <input id="RST">Active high reset signal</input>
        <output id="Hours" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit output representing the current hours</output>
        <output id="Mins" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit output representing the current minutes</output>
        <output id="Secs" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit output representing the current seconds</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="MAX_SECONDS">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_MINUTES">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_HOURS">23</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="Secs_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit register to store the current seconds value</reg>
        <reg id="Mins_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit register to store the current minutes value</reg>
        <reg id="Hours_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit register to store the current hours value</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The implementation of the calendar module consists of three always blocks that update the values of seconds, minutes, and hours based on the clock signal and reset signal.
    The first always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge CLK) or the positive edge of the reset signal (posedge RST). It checks if the reset signal is active (RST) and sets the seconds value (Secs) to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it wraps around and sets the seconds value to 0. Otherwise, it increments the seconds value by 1.
    The second always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the minutes value (Mins). If the reset signal is active, it sets the minutes value to 0. If both the minutes and seconds values are 59, it wraps around and sets the minutes value to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it increments the minutes value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the minutes value unchanged.
    The third always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the hours value (Hours). If the reset signal is active, it sets the hours value to 0. If the hours, minutes, and seconds values are all at their maximum (23, 59, and 59 respectively), it wraps around and sets the hours value to 0. If the minutes and seconds values are both 59, it increments the hours value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the hours value unchanged.
    </implementation>

<module id="adder_32bit">
    <description>Implement a carry-lookahead 32-bit adder.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="S" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit output representing the sum of A and B.</output>
        <output id="C32">Carry-out output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter>Carry</parameter>
        <parameter>Carry lookahead logic is used to calculate the carry outputs based on the generate and propagate signals.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    The carry-lookahead adder is a fast arithmetic unit that computes the sum of two 32-bit operands, A and B, producing a 32-bit output S and a carry-out C32.
    The adder is organized into four 16-bit CLA blocks, each responsible for calculating the sum of 16 bits of A and B. The carry-in for each block is computed based on the carry-out of the previous block.
    The structure of the adder is as follows:
    1. The first CLA block computes the sum of the least significant 16 bits of A and B, using a carry-in of 0.
    2. Each subsequent CLA block computes the sum of the next 16 bits of A and B, using the carry-out of the previous block as the carry-in.
    3. The carry-out C32 of the entire 32-bit adder is assigned as the carry-out of the last CLA block.
    The outputs S and C32 represent the sum of A and B and the carry-out of the addition operation, respectively.
    </implementation>
</module>

module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);
    // State register used to control the state of the waveform generation.
    reg state;
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (~rst_n) begin
            state <= 1'b0;  // Reset state to 0
            wave <= 5'd0;   // Reset wave to 0
        end else begin
            case (state)
                1'b0: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd31) begin
                        state <= 1'b1;  // Transition to state 1 if wave reaches 31
                    end else begin
                        wave <= wave + 1;  // Increment wave
                    end
                end
                1'b1: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd0) begin
                        state <= 1'b0;  // Transition back to state 0 if wave reaches 0
                    end else begin
                        wave <= wave - 1;  // Decrement wave
                    end
                end
            endcase
        end
    end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave signal generator module that generates a waveform by incrementing and decrementing a 5-bit signal named "wave". The waveform cycles between 0 and 31, which is incremented or decremented by 1.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal representing the generated waveform.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="state">State register used to control the state of the waveform generation.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state">State register used to control the state of the waveform generation.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal representing the generated waveform.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module includes a register, state, used to control the state of the waveform generation.
        The module includes a register, wave, with a width of 5 bits, which represents the generated waveform.
        The state and wave registers are updated in the always block, triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).
        On reset, indicated by ~rst_n, the state register is cleared to 0, and the wave register is cleared to 0.
        The waveform generation is controlled using a case statement based on the current state:
        If the state is 0, the waveform (wave) is incremented by 1.
        If the waveform reaches 31 (wave == 31), the state is transitioned to 1.
        If the state is 1, the waveform is decremented by 1.
        If the waveform reaches 0 (wave == 0), the state is transitioned back to 0.
        The waveform generation continues to cycle between 0 and 31 based on the state transitions.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_pipe_64bit">
    <description>Implement a module of a 64-bit ripple carry adder, which includes several registers to enable the pipeline stages. The output result is available on the result port, and the o_en = 1 indicates the availability of the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active low reset signal</input>
        <input id="i_en">Enable signal for addition operation</input>
        <input id="adda" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand A</input>
        <input id="addb" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand B</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[64:0]">65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb.</output>
        <output id="o_en">Output enable signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="PIPELINE_STAGES">Number of pipeline stages, which is 3.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <reg id="i_en_reg" description="Enable signal for addition operation">Set i_en_reg to 0.</reg>
            <reg id="adda_reg" width_description="[63:0]" description="64-bit input operand A">Initialize adda_reg to 0.</reg>
            <reg id="addb_reg" width_description="[63:0]" description="64-bit input operand B">Initialize addb_reg to 0.</reg>
            <reg id="o_en_reg" description="Output enable signal">Set o_en_reg to 0.</reg>
            <reg id="result_reg" width_description="[64:0]" description="65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb">Initialize result_reg to 0.</reg>
        </sequential_logic>
        <combinational_logic>
            <assign description="Addition operation for pipeline stage 0">sum_reg_0 = adda_reg_0 + addb_reg_0 + carry_reg_0.</assign>
            <assign description="Addition operation for pipeline stage 1">sum_reg_1 = adda_reg_1 + addb_reg_1 + carry_reg_1.</assign>
            <assign description="Addition operation for pipeline stage 2">sum_reg_2 = adda_reg_2 + addb_reg_2 + carry_reg_2.</assign>
            <assign description="Addition operation for pipeline stage 3">sum_reg_3 = adda_reg_3 + addb_reg_3 + carry_reg_3.</assign>
        </combinational_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <always description="Behavioral description of sequential logic">
                if (rst_n == 0) begin
                    i_en_reg <= 0;
                    adda_reg <= 0;
                    addb_reg <= 0;
                    o_en_reg <= 0;
                    result_reg <= 0;
                end else begin
                    i_en_reg <= i_en;
                    adda_reg <= adda;
                    addb_reg <= addb;
                    o_en_reg <= o_en;
                    result_reg <= result;
                end
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Design a pulse detection unit. The input signal data_in is triggered by clk. When data_in changes from 0 to 1 to 0 (3 cycles), it is considered a pulse. The indicating signal data_out is 1 at the end of the pulse, and returns to 0 until the next pulse. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). 1-bit input signal. Output signal indicating the presence of pulses. Idle state. State 1. State 2. State 3. State register.

Declare a register state to keep track of the current state of the pulse detection process.

Inside an always block, sensitive to the positive edge of the clk signal and the negative edge of the rst_n signal, implement the logic for pulse detection and output generation.

In the reset condition (~rst_n), set the state register to the initial state and set the data_out output to 0, indicating no pulse.

Continue the detection process for the remaining states. For each state, check the value of data_in and transition to the next state accordingly.

If the current state satisfies the last state of a "pulse", set data_out to 1, indicating the end of a pulse. Otherwise, set data_out to 0.

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate input data in 4 cycles and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WAIT_CYCLES">Number of cycles to wait before outputting the accumulated result.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="count">Counter to keep track of the number of input data received.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="sum">Accumulated sum of input data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data_out_reg">Register to hold the output data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="valid_out_reg">Register to hold the output valid signal.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential>
            <if>
                <condition>
                    <and>
                        <equal>
                            <read id="valid_in"/>
                            <const>1'b1</const>
                        </equal>
                        <equal>
                            <read id="count"/>
                            <const>"0</const>
                        </equal>
                    </and>
                </condition>
                <then>
                    <assign>
                        <read id="sum"/> = 0;
                        <read id="count"/> = 0;
                    </assign>
                </then>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>
                    <and>
                        <equal>
                            <read id="valid_in"/>
                            <const>1'b1</const>
                        </equal>
                        <less_than>
                            <read id="count"/>
                            <const>"3</const>
                        </less_than>
                    </and>
                </condition>
                <then>
                    <assign>
                        <read id="sum"/> = <read id="sum"/> + <read id="data_in"/>;
                        <read id="count"/> = <read id="count"/> + 1;
                        <read id="valid_out_reg"/> = 0;
                    </assign>
                </then>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>
                    <and>
                        <equal>
                            <read id="valid_in"/>
                            <const>1'b1</const>
                        </equal>
                        <equal>
                            <read id="count"/>
                            <const>"4</const>
                        </equal>
                    </and>
                </condition>
                <then>
                    <assign>
                        <read id="data_out_reg"/> = <read id="sum"/> + <read id="data_in"/>;
                        <read id="sum"/> = 0;
                        <read id="count"/> = 0;
                        <read id="valid_out_reg"/> = 1;
                    </assign>
                </then>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>
                    <or>
                        <equal>
                            <read id="valid_out_reg"/>
                            <const>1'b1</const>
                        </equal>
                        <equal>
                            <read id="valid_out_reg"/>
                            <const>1'b0</const>
                        </equal>
                    </or>
                </condition>
                <then>
                    <assign>
                        <read id="valid_out_reg"/> = 0;
                    </assign>
                </then>
            </if>
        </sequential>
    </implementation>
</module>

module JC_counter
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n
);
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end else begin
        if (Q[0] == 0) begin
            Q <= {Q[63:1], 1'b1};
        end else begin
            Q <= {Q[63:1], 1'b0};
        end
    end
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit Johnson counter (torsional ring counter).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">Current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">64</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    The counter has N bits (default 64).
    On every rising edge of the clock, the counter value is updated.
    If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the counter value (Q) is set to 0.
    Otherwise, if the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 0, the counter increments by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 1 at the most significant bit position.
    If the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 1, the counter decrements by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 0 at the most significant bit position.
    </implementation>
</module>

module RAM
#(
    parameter WIDTH = 6,
    parameter DEPTH = 8
)
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0] read_data
);
    reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM [DEPTH-1:0];
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            for (integer i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                RAM[i] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
            end
        end else if (write_en) begin
            RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
    end
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end else if (read_en) begin
            read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
        end else begin
            read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end
    end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. 6 8 Read data register. Write data register. Write enable signal. Read enable signal. Active-low reset signal. Clock signal. Temporary register.

Define a 8-bit RAM array with a width of 6 bits, initialized to 000000.

Use two always blocks to trigger on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

On reset, clear all memory locations in the RAM array to 0.

If the write enable signal (write_en) is active, write the data (write_data) to the RAM array at the specified address (write_addr).

On reset, clear the read_data register to 0.

If the read enable signal (read_en) is active, assign the data at the specified address (read_addr) in the RAM array to the read_data register.

Otherwise, clear the read_data register to 0.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a right shifter. The module performs an 8-bit right shift on a 1-bit input by first initializing the q register to 0. On each rising edge of the clock, the module shifts the contents of the q register to the right by one bit and inserts the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register. Clock. Input. Output. None. sequential_logic q is initialized to 0.

Initialize the register q to 0 using the initial statement.

initial begin q = 8'b00000000; end

On each rising edge of the clock, shift the contents of the q register to the right by one bit using the >> operator.

always @(posedge clk) begin q <= (q >> 1); end

Insert the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register.

q[7] <= d;

The value of the register q is assigned to the output port q.

assign q = q;

samples.xml_description:
Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. The clock clk_a is responsible for: input data_in is refer to clock a. enable signal data_en is refer to clock a. The clock clk_b is responsible for the enable signal data_en delays two cycles, that is, two D flip-flops. This is done with reference to clock b. And the data is finally output dataout, and the output refers to clock b. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles. If the spec mentions "instantiate X", "use sub-module X", or "hierarchical", describe the equivalent inline behavior. Do NOT describe instantiation of sub-modules in XML; write the algorithm directly. Example: "design an 8-bit adder and instantiate twice" → describe as "implement 16-bit addition inline using a single assign statement". Clock signal A. Clock signal B. Active-low reset signal A. Active-low reset signal B. Input data signal. Input enable signal. Output data signal. Parameter Description is optional. type="sequential_logic" id="data_reg">Description of the data_reg logic here. type="sequential_logic" id="en_data_reg">Description of the en_data_reg logic here. type="sequential_logic" id="en_clap_one">Description of the en_clap_one logic here. type="sequential_logic" id="en_clap_two">Description of the en_clap_two logic here. Task Description here.

Register data_reg:

Reset: When arstn is 0, data_reg is 0.

Update: On the positive edge of clk_a, data_reg is updated to the value of data_in.

Register en_data_reg:

Reset: When arstn is 0, en_data_reg is 0.

Update: On the positive edge of clk_a, en_data_reg is updated to the value of data_en.

Register en_clap_one:

Reset: When brstn is 0, en_clap_one is 0.

Update: On the positive edge of clk_b, en_clap_one is updated to the value of en_data_reg.

Register en_clap_two:

Reset: When brstn is 0, en_clap_two is 0.

Update: On the positive edge of clk_b, en_clap_two is updated to the value of en_clap_one.

Output dataout:

Reset: When brstn is 0, dataout is 0.

Update: On the positive edge of clk_b, if en_clap_two is 1, dataout is updated to the value of data_reg.

Update: On the positive edge of clk_b, if en_clap_two is 0, dataout is updated to the value of dataout.
